page: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a general-purpose, network and EBS-optimized server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture with an Intel Xeon 8252C processor running at a high clock speed of 4.5 GHz. The instance provides 48 dedicated vCPUs (24 physical cores with hyper-threading) and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, relying instead on network-attached storage.

Benchmark data reveals strong memory performance, with the Passmark memory score placing in the top tier (top 10%). Large-block uncached memory read and write bandwidth tests both rank in the top 25%, reaching 232.82 GB/sec and 97.66 GB/sec respectively. CPU performance is solid, highlighted by a strong showing in the Passmark CPU extended instructions test and single-threaded gzip compression. General compute, database operations, and LLM prompt processing benchmarks consistently score in the average range, though LLM text generation speeds represent a relative bottleneck, falling into the bottom 25%.

Equipped with a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth, the m5zn.12xlarge is designed for high-throughput networking and EBS-optimized storage tasks. From a cost-efficiency perspective, the instance offers high resource density and high-frequency dedicated compute without the overhead of local storage or GPU hardware. This profile makes it highly suitable for network-intensive workloads, high-frequency financial simulations, enterprise application servers, and database operations that demand rapid memory access and high network throughput.
description: Amazon Web Services m5zn.12xlarge is a high-performance, general-purpose server instance optimized for network and EBS throughput. Powered by the Nitro hypervisor, it features an Intel Xeon 8252C processor with 48 dedicated vCPUs running at 4.5 GHz and 192.0 GB of DDR4 memory. While it lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, it delivers a 100 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Benchmark results show top-tier memory performance, with Passmark memory scores in the top 10% and strong large-block memory bandwidth. General compute, database operations, and LLM prompt processing perform in the average tier, while LLM text generation is a relative weakness. This hardware profile is highly suited for network-intensive applications, database hosting, and high-frequency compute tasks requiring fast memory access.

tagline: A high-frequency, network-optimized virtual server delivering dedicated compute cores and top-tier memory performance for throughput-demanding enterprise applications.

192.0 GB of DDR4 memory with top-tier Passmark memory performance
